summary: Added generated-art Chapter Gate Heatfield to the selected Path Map chapter's Chapter Gate Stack so blockers, review gates, and local Gate Radar notes read as a tactical scan instead of only a list.

queries: UX design risk heatmap dashboard blockers visualization patterns
game UI radar mission control dashboard visual design references
visualizing project blockers milestones dashboard UX game-like
pattern_notes: Operational dashboards should keep a glanceable overview near the detailed records so users can move from state to evidence quickly.
Milestone and timeline UX patterns support detail-on-demand when long-running work keeps expanding.
Radar and mission-map game UI references work best here when the pings are data-derived and backed by explicit labels.

generated_asset.prompt_summary: Premium dark sci-fi blocker radar heatfield dashboard texture with teal scan rings, amber/coral pressure pings, no text, no logos, and no characters.

ui_contract: Render a path-chapter-gate-heatfield panel inside Chapter Gate Stack.
Use generated path-chapter-gate-heatfield-20260618.png as the scan backdrop.
Compute heatfield pings from pathChapterGateStackItems so future lanes inherit the same blocker visualization.
Expose explicit ping labels, severity, and pressure values in the heat key.
Keep Q, COM, and Gate Radar actions browser-local and unchanged.
Register the generated texture in the Visual Asset Vault system assets.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_gate_stack.py -q failed before implementation on missing function pathChapterGateHeatfieldItems.
red_green.green: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_gate_stack.py -q passed after generated asset, component, styles, docs, cache token, and Asset Vault wiring.

browser_green_route.evidence: Rendered Chapter Gate Heatfield with generated backdrop path-chapter-gate-heatfield-20260618.png, 3 data pings, 3 heat-key cells, active gateHeatfieldSweep animation, Chapter Gate Stack cards/actions, Asset Vault registration, zero bad rectangles, zero horizontal overflow, and zero console errors. Clicking the Gate Stack Q control changed the local staged command button to OK while keeping the heatfield rendered.

responsive.evidence: Browser sweep at 390x820, 820x900, 1280x900, and 1600x1000 kept the heatfield visible with 3 pings, 3 heat-key cells, zero clipped rectangles, zero text-cell overflow, zero horizontal overflow, and zero console errors.
